message:
feat(io): add typed integer write methods to AsyncWriteExt and promote futures-lite to runtime dep

Mirrors the AsyncReadExt integer method expansion with a symmetric set
of 22 write methods on AsyncWriteExt for all standard integer and float
types in both byte orders: write_u16, write_u16_le, write_i16,
write_i16_le, write_u32, write_u32_le, write_i32, write_i32_le,
write_u64, write_u64_le, write_i64, write_i64_le, write_u128,
write_u128_le, write_i128, write_i128_le, write_f32, write_f32_le,
write_f64, write_f64_le. Also adds write_i8 and a base write() method.

Implementation uses a write_int_trait_method! macro that generates trait
methods returning futures with internal fixed-size byte buffers filled
via to_be_bytes/to_le_bytes. Each future polls write until all bytes
are flushed.

Also includes minor formatting fix in read_ext.rs (multiline assert
macro expansion for f32/f64 tests).

Cargo.toml: moves futures-lite from dev-dependencies to dependencies
since the web handler async shim now uses it at runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
